title: Gomphandra fuliginea
taxonomy: Gomphandra fuliginea is classified within the family Stemonuraceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the Stemonuraceae family. This family is part of the order Aquifoliales, which is a small order of flowering plants. The order Aquifoliales is within the clade asterids, a large and diverse clade of flowering plants. The clade asterids is part of the larger clade angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. Gomphandra fuliginea's classification also includes its position within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Gomphandra fuliginea, a species of flowering plant, has been found to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. intro: Gomphandra fuliginea is a woody perennial plant native to the tropical forests of Asia, belonging to the family Stemonuraceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Gomphandra fuliginea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
